question.text: In a price-time priority system, three buy orders arrive: Order A at Rs. 101.50 (10:01 AM), Order B at Rs. 101.70 (10:02 AM), Order C at Rs. 101.50 (10:00 AM). A sell order at Rs. 101.50 arrives. Which order gets filled first?

answer.text: Order B, because it has the highest bid price
Order A, because it arrived before Order C at the same price
Order C, because it arrived earliest at Rs. 101.50

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Price-time priority: First, the highest bid gets priority. Order B (Rs. 101.70) has the highest price and gets matched first, even though it arrived later. **The Trap:** Order C has time priority at Rs. 101.50, but price priority trumps time priority. Order A has neither the best price nor earliest time at that price. Simultaneous fill doesn't apply in price-time priority. **Cross-Exam Context:** Price-time priority is the universal matching principle tested in all exchange-traded product exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember: PRICE first, then TIME. A better price always beats an earlier timestamp at a worse price.
